summary: In this episode, Bhartendra Singh sits down with renowned wealth expert Vijay Mantri to decode the truths most investors miss: why “bad returns” are often the best returns for SIP investors, how to spot a real financial advisor, and why real estate feels real but isn’t liquid. We also break down Gold vs Titan (20x vs 500x), the equity earnings engine, and practical frameworks to invest with discipline.

chapters.label: Why Dont People Have Clarity on Investing ?
Information vs discipline (Indian philosophy, guru–shishya lens)
How to find a good advisor (risk is more important return)
Beyond past performance: what to actually check in mutual funds
HDFC Flexi Cap: returns vs the real journey (drawdowns)
Debt funds vs FD: YTM is more important historical returns
Small/Midcap outperformance & caution on recency bias
Media headlines, fear & greed loops - Gold Vs Equity
Gold vs Titan (20x vs 500x) and productive assets. Truth about Gold Returns
Equity is most predictable long term (inflation is the real risk)
You use 160 listed companies daily, be an owner, not just a user
Real estate’s hidden risks & illiquidity - It is not so easy to sell a real estate.
Long-term Sensex stats: probability of positive returns rises with time
SIP mechanics: AMC–Trust structure & tax nuance
Rupee-Cost Averaging math: why drawdowns help SIPs
What top family offices learned (balanced advantage funds)

tool.reason: Gold and silver are a slice, not the plate. See where they fit in your full mix.

seo.title: Bad Returns Are the BEST Returns | Vijai Mantri on Advisors, Mutual Fund SIP, Gold vs Equity
seo.description: In this episode, Bhartendra Singh sits down with renowned wealth expert Vijay Mantri to decode the truths most investors miss: why “bad returns” are often

takeaways: A good advisor leads with risk and mentions returns last; if the pitch opens with how much you will make, treat that as the red flag.
Chasing past performance sets you up to be disappointed; what matters more is what you check next, like YTM on debt funds instead of just historical returns.
Great funds still come with rough journeys: the HDFC Flexi Cap illustration shows strong outcomes arrived alongside real, stomach-testing drawdowns.
Gold and Titan are made of the same gold, yet Titan compounded far more, a reminder that productive assets do work that a metal sitting in a locker cannot.
For a SIP investor, a falling NAV is not the enemy; rupee-cost averaging means the down phase is often when your money is buying the most units.
